Activity 04

Community Building

Privacy advocacy is stronger when advocates know each other. ODIPA builds the connective tissue — summits, meetups, forums, and a volunteer network that turns individual commitment into collective impact.

Free meetups
Overview

ODIPA's Community Building program creates the infrastructure for a sustained privacy advocacy movement. We host an annual Privacy Summit, local meetups, online discussion groups, and regional events that bring together attorneys, technologists, policymakers, students, and engaged citizens. Our volunteer network connects skilled professionals with the programs that need their expertise. All community events are free to attend — we believe financial barriers should never limit participation in civic advocacy.

What We Do

Annual Privacy Summit

A full-day conference bringing together advocates, technologists, attorneys, policymakers, and citizens for talks, panels, and workshops. All attendees pay a conference fee — this is the fee-based portion of this program (2% of total activity).

Local Meetups

In-person gatherings in Los Angeles and partner cities for privacy professionals and advocates to connect and share updates.

Online Discussion Forums

Virtual discussion groups covering current events in privacy, peer questions, and collaborative advocacy coordination.

Regional Events

Networking events hosted in different cities to expand ODIPA's community reach beyond the Los Angeles area.

Speaker Series

Featured speakers — attorneys, regulators, researchers, and industry leaders — sharing expertise with the community.

Volunteer Network

A structured volunteer program connecting skilled professionals with ODIPA programs that need their expertise.

Who We Serve
Privacy Professionals

Attorneys, compliance officers, and technologists who want to connect with peers and contribute to advocacy.

Engaged Citizens

Anyone passionate about digital rights who wants to participate in a community of advocates.

Students & Researchers

Academics and students exploring privacy as a field and looking for a community to learn from.

Policymakers

Legislators and regulators who participate in ODIPA forums to hear directly from consumers and experts.

Year 1 Targets
1
Annual summit
12+
Planned local meetups
24+
Planned online forums
100%
Free to attend
Common Questions

Where are ODIPA events held?

Our primary hub is Los Angeles, where most in-person events are held. We also host virtual events accessible to anyone nationally, and regional events in partner cities.

How do I join the community?

Subscribe to our newsletter to receive event announcements. All public events are free and open to anyone — no membership required.

Can I speak or present at an ODIPA event?

Yes. We welcome proposals for the Speaker Series and Summit from qualified advocates, researchers, and practitioners. Contact us at events@odipa.org.

Ready to Get Involved?

Join ODIPA and help protect digital privacy for everyone.

Join the CommunityVolunteer

Organization for Digital Information Privacy & Awareness

Educating consumers and organizations about digital information privacy — advocating for practices and policies that protect people in an increasingly data-driven world.

ODIPA is funded through individual donations, corporate sponsorships, and foundation grants.

Privacy Newsletter

Privacy digest: breach alerts, new laws, research releases.

No spam, ever. Unsubscribe any time. We never sell your data. Privacy Policy
